No Poverty.reason: The course content focuses purely on philosophical and theological arguments, with no connection to economic or social issues related to poverty.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course is entirely focused on theological and philosophical concepts, with no direct or indirect links to food security or hunger.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course content is strictly academic philosophy and theology, with no connection to physical or mental health services or outcomes.

Quality Education.reason: As a university-level philosophy course, it directly contributes to quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and fosters critical thinking and philosophical insights (relevant to Target 4.7).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course focuses on abstract philosophical problems, not economic development, employment, or labor conditions.

Life on Land.reason: The course content is abstract philosophy and theology, with no connection to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.
